messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching]  help!
messages.body_plain:
Forwarded message: From: "Amir Sadoddin" <amir.sadoddin at gmail.com>
> 
> Hello, I was wondering how I can have outlier detection for hydrologic data
> in particular peak flow data in R.
 

Dear Dr. Sadoddin,

This list is for people using R in introductory statistics courses so
your question is a bit off-topic.  You may well get some useful
response, but you might get even MORE useful response from a more
technical list.  Here you you will find many people like myself who are
not expert in R (nor hydrology).  


------->  First-time AP Stats. teacher?  Help is on the way! See

 http://courses.ncssm.edu/math/Stat_Inst/Stats2007/Bob%20Hayden/Relief.html
	    
  Robert W. Hayden in the old library at  212 Main Street (P. O. Box 450)
  North Troy, VT 05859  phone (802) 988-2587  web site http://statland.org/      
  email  bob statland.org  (add your own "@" and save me some spam)
messages.body_snippet:
Forwarded message: From: "Amir Sadoddin" <amir.sadoddin at gmail.com>
> 
> Hello, I was wondering how I can have outlier detection for hydrologic data
> in particular peak flow data in R.
 

Dear Dr.

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] R-sig-teaching Digest, Vol 13, Issue 2
messages.body_plain:
Amir,

Although I am unfamiliar with "hydrologic data in particular peak 
flow", generally outliers can be visualized using ?boxplot(). Also 
take a look at help(outlier.test, package=car).

Best regards,
Tony


--On Tuesday, February 12, 2008 12:00 PM +0100
